title: Check yourself
questions.0.text: A counter accumulates sales: let sum = 0; then sum += 10; then sum =+ 5; then sum += 3. What is sum at the end?
questions.0.options.3: The code breaks on the reversed signs
questions.0.why: The line sum =+ 5 does not accumulate, it replaces — the ten disappears and counting restarts from 5. Then 3 is added. Valid code, silent loss.
questions.1.text: The quantity field contains "12 pcs". Which one returns the number 12?
questions.1.why: parseInt reads from the left while it sees digits, then stops. Number wants the whole text to be a number and returns NaN. trim only removes spaces — the result is still text.
questions.2.text: const n = Number("coffee"); Which is true about n?
questions.2.options.1: The code already broke at Number
questions.2.options.3: n * 2 gives NaN, with nothing complaining
questions.2.why: NaN is not an error, it is a value. Every sum with it gives NaN again and spreads downwards quietly. Comparing with equality never works — that is what Number.isNaN is for.
questions.3.text: You have 20 lev, a cup costs 3. Which one works out how many WHOLE cups you can buy?
questions.3.why: 20 divided by 3 gives 6.67. Rounding to the nearest and rounding up both give 7 — and there is nothing to pay for the seventh cup with. Rounding down cuts off the remainder and gives 6.
